name: Chicken Biryani
description: Fragrant basmati rice layered with spiced chicken. The undisputed king of delivery orders.

name: Butter Chicken
description: Tender chicken in a rich, creamy tomato gravy. The one everyone agrees on.

name: Dal Makhani
description: Slow-cooked black lentils in buttery tomato gravy. Comfort in a bowl.

name: Paneer Tikka
description: Smoky charred paneer with peppers and spices. The vegetarian crowd-pleaser.

name: Chole Bhature
description: Spiced chickpeas with fluffy fried bread. Weekend morning energy.

name: Masala Dosa
description: Crispy rice crepe stuffed with spiced potato filling. Lighter than it looks.

name: Palak Paneer
description: Cottage cheese in a smooth spinach gravy. The one that makes you feel responsible.

name: Mutton Biryani
description: Slow-cooked mutton layered with saffron rice. For when you mean business.

name: Pav Bhaji
description: Spiced mashed vegetables with butter-toasted bread. Mumbai on a plate.

name: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Grilled chicken in a bold, tangy masala sauce. Never disappoints.

name: Veg Biryani
description: Fragrant rice with seasonal vegetables and whole spices. Underrated.

name: Prawn Curry
description: Fresh prawns in a coastal coconut-tomato gravy. Best when someone else cooks it.

name: Rajma Chawal
description: Red kidney beans in thick gravy over steamed rice. The ultimate comfort food.

name: Chicken Kebab
description: Minced or whole chicken pieces off the grill. No utensils required.

name: Idli Sambar
description: Steamed rice cakes with tangy lentil soup. Light, filling, zero regrets.

name: Tandoori Chicken
description: Whole chicken marinated in yoghurt and spices, cooked in a clay oven.

name: Aloo Paratha
description: Stuffed flatbread with spiced potato, served with curd and pickle. Breakfast that eats like a meal.

name: Fish Curry
description: Fresh fish in a tangy, spiced gravy. Coastal India delivered to your door.

name: Chicken Roll
description: Spiced chicken wrapped in a flaky paratha. The original grab-and-go.

name: Samosa
description: Crispy fried pastry stuffed with spiced potato and peas. Two is never enough.

name: Margherita Pizza
description: Tomato, mozzarella, basil. The one that started it all.

name: Pepperoni Pizza
description: Classic American-style pizza with spicy pepperoni. Always a crowd-pleaser.

name: Pasta Arrabbiata
description: Penne in a spicy tomato sauce. Simple, fast, satisfying.

name: Pasta Alfredo
description: Fettuccine in a rich, creamy white sauce. Comfort food, Italian edition.

name: Barbeque Chicken Pizza
description: Smoky BBQ sauce, chicken, onions on a crispy base. Better than expected every time.

name: Lasagne
description: Layered pasta with meat sauce and béchamel. Takes effort — which is why delivery is better.

name: Garlic Bread
description: Buttery, garlicky toasted bread. The side dish that becomes the main event.

name: Classic Burger
description: Beef patty, lettuce, tomato, cheese in a brioche bun. The benchmark.

name: Chicken Burger
description: Crispy or grilled chicken in a soft bun. The one you order when you're pretending to be healthy.

name: Loaded Fries
description: Fries topped with cheese, jalapeños, and sauce. A meal pretending to be a side.

name: Hot Dog
description: A classic frankfurter in a soft bun with mustard and ketchup.

name: Chicken Wings
description: Crispy fried wings tossed in hot sauce or BBQ glaze. Best eaten with someone who doesn't judge.

name: Grilled Chicken Sandwich
description: Lean, grilled chicken in a toasted bun. The choice that feels responsible.

name: Veg Hakka Noodles
description: Stir-fried noodles with vegetables in soy and chilli sauce. Indian Chinese at its best.

name: Chicken Fried Rice
description: Wok-tossed rice with chicken and egg. The delivery staple that never lets you down.

name: Manchurian
description: Deep-fried balls in a tangy, spicy Manchurian sauce. India's most beloved Chinese dish.

name: Schezwan Noodles
description: Noodles tossed in a fiery Schezwan sauce. For when you want heat with your meal.

name: Dim Sum
description: Steamed dumplings filled with vegetables or chicken. Light enough to order guilt-free.

name: Honey Chilli Potato
description: Crispy potato strips in sweet chilli glaze. The starter that becomes the meal.

name: Chicken Lollipop
description: Marinated chicken winglets deep-fried and sauced. Finger food taken seriously.

name: Steamed Momos
description: Soft dumplings filled with spiced vegetables or chicken. Delhi's favourite street food.

name: Fried Momos
description: Crispy on the outside, juicy on the inside. Better than steamed and you know it.

name: Tandoori Momos
description: Smoky, charred momos with a spicy coating. The glow-up of a classic.

name: Sushi Roll
description: Rice and fish wrapped in seaweed. Available in metros — worth it when done well.

name: Ramen
description: Rich broth with noodles, egg, and toppings. A bowl that takes an hour to make and 10 minutes to finish.

name: Chicken Caesar Salad
description: Romaine, grilled chicken, croutons, Caesar dressing. The salad that actually fills you up.

name: Club Sandwich
description: Triple-decker sandwich with chicken, egg, lettuce, and cheese. A full meal between bread.

name: Grilled Sandwich
description: Toasted sandwich with cheese and vegetables. Simple, quick, satisfying.

name: Chicken Shawarma
description: Spiced chicken wrapped in flatbread with garlic sauce. The late-night champion.

name: Falafel Wrap
description: Crispy chickpea fritters in a flatbread with hummus and salad. Vegetarian and proud.

name: Hummus with Pita
description: Creamy chickpea dip with warm flatbread. The starter that becomes a snack becomes a meal.

name: Burrito Bowl
description: Rice, beans, grilled chicken, salsa, and guacamole in a bowl. Tex-Mex has arrived in India.

name: Nachos
description: Tortilla chips loaded with cheese, jalapeños, and salsa. Sharing optional.

name: Quesadilla
description: Grilled flatbread stuffed with cheese and chicken. The satisfying crunch is half the appeal.

name: Pad Thai
description: Stir-fried rice noodles with peanuts, egg, and tamarind. Thailand's most exported dish.

name: Green Curry
description: Coconut milk curry with vegetables or chicken and Thai basil. Fragrant and fierce.

name: Tom Yum Soup
description: Spicy and sour broth with mushrooms and lemongrass. Hot, tangy, and clearing your sinuses.

name: Veg Sandwich
description: Fresh vegetables, cheese, and chutney between toasted bread. Light and reliable.

name: Paneer Roll
description: Spiced paneer wrapped in a flaky paratha. The vegetarian answer to the chicken roll.

name: Egg Roll
description: Egg-coated paratha wrapped around spiced filling. Kolkata's greatest export.

name: Vada Pav
description: Spiced potato fritter in a soft bun with chutneys. Mumbai's soul food.

name: Dahi Puri
description: Crispy puris filled with potato, yoghurt, and chutneys. Street food that requires full attention.

name: Bhel Puri
description: Puffed rice with vegetables, chutneys, and sev. The snack that disappears in minutes.

name: Chowmein
description: Stir-fried noodles, Indian street style. Faster, spicier, and more satisfying than the original.

name: Chocolate Brownie
description: Dense, fudgy chocolate brownie. The one everyone wants but nobody orders first.

name: Gulab Jamun
description: Soft milk-solid dumplings soaked in rose syrup. The dessert that ends every Indian meal.

name: Rasmalai
description: Soft cottage cheese patties in sweetened saffron milk. Lighter than it sounds.

name: Cheesecake
description: Creamy, rich cheesecake on a biscuit base. The one dessert that justifies skipping dinner.

name: Waffles
description: Crispy outside, fluffy inside, with toppings of your choice. Breakfast food that became a dessert somehow.

name: Chocolate Cake
description: Moist, layered chocolate cake with ganache frosting. You don't need a reason.

name: Kulfi
description: Dense, creamy Indian ice cream in pistachio or mango. Summer in every bite.

name: Cold Coffee
description: Chilled blended coffee with milk and ice cream. The drink that's basically dessert.

name: Mango Lassi
description: Thick yoghurt-based mango drink. The one thing that cools you down after anything spicy.

name: Masala Chai
description: Spiced Indian tea with milk. The answer to most problems.

name: Chicken Soup
description: Clear broth with shredded chicken and vegetables. The only acceptable sick-day order.

name: Quinoa Bowl
description: Quinoa with roasted vegetables and a tahini dressing. Feels virtuous, tastes surprisingly good.

name: Grilled Fish
description: Lemon-herb grilled fish fillet. Light enough to not feel bad about dessert.

name: Oats Bowl
description: Warm oats with fruits and nuts. Breakfast food for people who have their life together.

name: Egg Bhurji
description: Spiced scrambled eggs with onions and tomatoes. The 11pm hero.

name: Maggi
description: Two-minute noodles, dressed up or plain. The universal late-night language.

name: Paratha with Curd
description: Plain or stuffed flatbread with cooling yoghurt. Simple, filling, never wrong.

name: Chicken Qorma
description: Slow-cooked chicken in a rich, aromatic yoghurt gravy. The kind of curry that tastes better the next day.

name: Pho
description: Fragrant rice noodle soup with herbs and your choice of protein. Light, warm, and deeply satisfying.

name: Spring Rolls
description: Crispy rolls filled with vegetables or chicken. The starter that disappears before the main arrives.

name: Dosa
description: Thin, crispy rice crepe served with sambar and chutneys. South India's gift to the world.
